title: polygala cuneata f. obovata
intro: Polygala cuneata f. obovata is a type of flowering plant species in the Polygalaceae family, commonly known as the 'Kaleekalee' or 'Kalee-kalee', native to Hawaii and the Marquesas Islands. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Polygala cuneata f. obovata are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping its roots and leaves in hot water, which is then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
